sections.paragraph:
to thrust, to push, to throw down, ftoßen, umftoßen. (Arabic دحا id., see Jeuhari in Schultens, on Job p. 1101; also de coitu, like ضرب and other verbs of thrusting, see דַּחֲוָה . Syriac and Ch. ܕܚܐܳ, דְּחָא id. The idea of thrusting, pushing, knocking, impelling, is found in many verbs, whose primary syllable is דח, as דּוּחַ, דָּחַח, דָּחַף, דָּחַק, נָדַח, دحب, دحم, دحر, דְּחַס, ܕܚܫ, compare similar families under the words דָּכַךְ and דָּקַק.) Psa 35:5 118:13 , דָּחֹה דְחִיתַנִי לִנְפֹּל “thou hast thrust at me that I might fall;” 140:5 . Psa 62:4, גָּדֵר הַדְּחוּיָה “an overturned wall.”

Niphal, pass. of Kal to be thrust away, Pro 14:32, “the wicked is driven away in his wickedness,” i.e. perishes, rushes to destruction. Compare יִדַּחוּ (prop. from דָּחַח), Jer 23:12. But the part. plur. constr. נִדְחֵי, as נִדְחֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל, is more correctly referred to נָדַח which see.

Pual, pret. דֹּחוּ “they are thrown down,” Ps. 36:13 .

Derivatives דְּחִי, מִדְחֶה and-
